patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 1/2] kernel/entry: add CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH to compile SUD out

version_history.subject: [PATCH 1/2] kernel/entry: add CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH to compile SUD out

series_items.subject: [PATCH 2/2] kernel/entry: add kernel.syscall_user_dispatch sysctl
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) to control whether userspace can arm Syscall User Dispatch (SUD). The default value is 1, allowing arming of SUD. Disarming SUD is always permitted. The sysctl is only present when the kernel is built with CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH. This patch also updates documentation and code to reflect the new sysctl.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option, CONFIG_SYSCALL_USER_DISPATCH, to make Syscall User Dispatch (SUD) an optional feature in the Linux kernel. SUD allows system calls to be redirected to userspace handlers and is required by native foreign-binary emulators like Wine/Proton. The patch introduces this new configuration option to allow minimal or high-security systems to disable SUD if needed, while still making it available for systems that require it.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v2] mm/vmstat: fold stranded per-cpu node stats when a node comes online

version_history.subject: [PATCH v2] mm/vmstat: fold stranded per-cpu node stats when a node comes online

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch addresses a long-standing issue where per-cpu node statistics are not properly folded when a node comes online, leading to permanent skew in the vmstat counters. The existing code zeroes out the per-cpu counters, causing a permanent loss of data. This patch folds the stranded deltas before the node rejoins the online set, ensuring accurate and consistent statistics.

review_comments.author: Andrew Morton
review_comments.summary: Raised concerns about the safety of folding stranded per-cpu node statistics during offlining, suggesting that it could lead to deadlocks or other issues. Also questioned the need for visiting every possible CPU.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sat, 27 Jun 2026 16:22:43 -0400 Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

> A per-node vmstat counter is pgdat->vm_stat[] plus per-cpu deltas.
> A balanced counter can sit split as global=+N / per-cpu=-N.
> 
> The folds reconciling the split only walk online nodes, so when
> try_offline_node() marks a node offline the per-cpu deltas are stranded.
> 
> A subsequent online resets the per-cpu area but not pgdat->vm_stat[],
> orphaning the +N permanently.  All NR_VM_NODE_STAT_ITEMS are affected.

Geeze, simple mistake, been there ten years...

> The existing code zeroes the per-cpu counters and causes a permanent
> skew. Fold the stranded deltas instead, before the node rejoins the
> online set. The node is not online yet and the hotplug lock is held,
> so the remote access to per-cpu values is safe.

Oh.  Shouldn't we be doing this during offlining?

> Discovered when node compaction hung for a nearly empty node, as the
> math to determine throttling broke.  Reproduced by repeated memory
> hotplug/unplug cycles on a node under pressure: NR_ISOLATED_ANON
> ratchets up and never returns to zero.
> 
> ...
>
> --- a/mm/mm_init.c
> +++ b/mm/mm_init.c
> @@ -1536,7 +1536,7 @@ void __ref free_area_init_core_hotplug(struct pglist_data *pgdat)
>  {
>  	int nid = pgdat->node_id;
>  	enum zone_type z;
> -	int cpu;
> +	int cpu, i;
>  
>  	pgdat_init_internals(pgdat);
>  
> @@ -1554,10 +1554,17 @@ void __ref free_area_init_core_hotplug(struct pglist_data *pgdat)
>  	pgdat->node_start_pfn = 0;
>  	pgdat->node_present_pages = 0;
>  
> -	for_each_online_cpu(cpu) {
> -		struct per_cpu_nodestat *p;
> +	/*
> +	 * Hot-unplug can leave per-cpu vmstat deltas unfolded (folders skip
> +	 * offline nodes) - reconcile this at online. Foreign access to counters
> +	 * is safe: the node is not online yet and we hold the hotplug lock.
> +	 */
> +	for_each_possible_cpu(cpu) {

That's a lot of CPUs

> +		struct per_cpu_nodestat *p = per_cpu_ptr(pgdat->per_cpu_nodestats, cpu);
>  
> -		p = per_cpu_ptr(pgdat->per_cpu_nodestats, cpu);
> +		for (i = 0; i < NR_VM_NODE_STAT_ITEMS; i++)

and that's a lot of items.

I guess the overall loop count won't be large enough to cause issues,
but it's large!

Perhaps there's some simple test we can do on the per_cpu_nodestat to
avoid the inner loop?  Perhaps might need to add a field for this?

btw, "for(int i..." is allowed nowadays.  It'll make this code nicer, IMO.

And... Sashiko seems to have found a pre-existing issue:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260627202243.758289-1-gourry@gourry.net

> +			if (p->vm_node_stat_diff[i])
> +				node_page_state_add(p->vm_node_stat_diff[i], pgdat, i);
>  		memset(p, 0, sizeof(*p));
>  	}

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Responded to Andrew Morton's concerns, explaining the difficulties of safely folding stranded per-cpu node statistics during offlining and the potential risks of visiting every possible CPU.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 04:10:07PM -0700, Andrew Morton wrote:
> 
> > The existing code zeroes the per-cpu counters and causes a permanent
> > skew. Fold the stranded deltas instead, before the node rejoins the
> > online set. The node is not online yet and the hotplug lock is held,
> > so the remote access to per-cpu values is safe.
> 
> Oh.  Shouldn't we be doing this during offlining?
>

I tried this first.  I was unable to convince myself there was a 
safe way to accomplish this.

1) sashiko pointed out we can't schedule_on_each_cpu while holding
   the hotplug lock because we'll re-take cpus_read_lock and cause
   a deadlock condition with cpu-hotplug.

2) I'm not sure we can do it after the hotplug lock as been dropped,
   at least not safely.  At the very least another hot-plug re-adding
   the node could start.  That just seemed like a bad path.

3) foreign cpu access to the per-cpu values are not atomic with
   respect to in-flight folds on the target cpu.  this_cpu_xchg
   and this_cpu_add are (i believe) only atomic wrt the cpu itself
   (can't be interrupted mid-exchange).

doing it before node_offline() has problems (in-flight folds),
doing it after node_offline() still *technically* carries the same
in-flight fold risk - just narrower (fold has to have started already).

I couldn't convince myself there wasn't still a race, so here we are.

> > +	for_each_possible_cpu(cpu) {
> 
> That's a lot of CPUs
> 

Unfortunately - cpus may have gone offline while the node was offline,
so we legitimately have to visit every *possible* cpu :[

> > +		struct per_cpu_nodestat *p = per_cpu_ptr(pgdat->per_cpu_nodestats, cpu);
> >  
> > -		p = per_cpu_ptr(pgdat->per_cpu_nodestats, cpu);
> > +		for (i = 0; i < NR_VM_NODE_STAT_ITEMS; i++)
> 
> and that's a lot of items.

I am aware :[.  I suppose we could vectorize the collection here on some
archs, but I try to avoid being clever where I can.

> 
> I guess the overall loop count won't be large enough to cause issues,
> but it's large!
> 
> Perhaps there's some simple test we can do on the per_cpu_nodestat to
> avoid the inner loop?  Perhaps might need to add a field for this?

Hadn't considered this, but maybe.  Will take a look.

> 
> btw, "for(int i..." is allowed nowadays.  It'll make this code nicer, IMO.
> 

aye aye o7

> And... Sashiko seems to have found a pre-existing issue:
> 	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260627202243.758289-1-gourry@gourry.net
> 

Will take a look, thanks!

~Gregory

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] mm/vmstat: flush per-cpu node stats when a node goes offline

version_history.subject: [PATCH] mm/vmstat: flush per-cpu node stats when a node goes offline

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch addresses a problem where per-cpu node stats become stranded when a node goes offline, causing inconsistent vmstat values. The solution involves flushing the per-cpu deltas before the node leaves the online set, ensuring that the global counters accurately reflect the system's state.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Identified a potential deadlock issue with concurrent cpu hotplug and the sync code. Realized that zeroing per-cpu stats at online time is the root cause of the problem, not the solution.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 03:31:07AM -0400, Gregory Price wrote:
>  
>  	/*
>  	 * all memory/cpu of this node are removed, we can offline this
> -	 * node now.
> +	 * node now.  Fold any pending per-cpu vmstat diffs into the global
> +	 * counters first: once the node leaves the online set the periodic
> +	 * fold skips it, orphaning the residual on a later online.
>  	 */
> +	sync_vm_stats();

Sashiko points out this can deadlock on concurrent cpu hotplug, which
after a quick look seems accurate.

Has also made me realize the sync code is also racy with cpu hotplug
as well as it iterates per-online-cpu.

There are probably more races like this out there.

Will need to give this a little more thought.

>  	node_set_offline(nid);
>  	unregister_node(nid);
>  }

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH] mm/vmstat: flush per-cpu node stats when a node goes offline
Re: [PATCH] mm/vmstat: flush per-cpu node stats when a node goes offline
